sources.summary: WiNDS describes NGC 6946 as an isolated low-inclination spiral with strong arms, high star formation, dwarf companions, and global residual-velocity perturbations likely including vertical flows.

quotes.quote: In Figure  fig:candidate_ngc6946 we focus on NGC 6946. This is a spiral galaxy classified as SABcd with a kinematical inclination of i 17^ , an optical radius of 10 kpc, and an estimated total mass of 1.2 10^ 11 M _ 2000MNRAS.319..821P . Optical and neutral hydrogen observations of NGC 6946 have revealed its strong spiral arms, a particularly high star formation rate, a nuclear starburst, and a weak bar 2000MNRAS.319..821P,2006ApJ...649..181S .

NGC 6946 is considered to be isolated 1988JBAA...98..316T . Using HI observations, previous studies have detected a dozen of low mass irregular dwarf galaxies companions 2000MNRAS.319..821P, 2000A&A...362..544K, 2005A&A...434..935K . However, at the present day NGC 6946 does not show any sign of undergoing strong direct gravitational interactions with its satellite population. On panels d) and e) we show the galaxy V _ res map, which reveals global and coherent perturbations, reaching amplitudes 35 \ km \ s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as a nearby face-on SABcd galaxy with several Type II supernovae, using two optimal events to derive mutually consistent tailored-EPM distances.

quotes.quote: By filtering the catalogue, we found that SN IIP pairs in four host galaxies met all the conditions that we described above: NGC 772, NGC 922, NGC 4303 (M 61), and NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:hosts ). To retrieve the data for the supernovae in these hosts, we made use of the OSC and WISeREP https://www.wiserep.org/ wiserep .

NGC 6946 NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:hosts ) is a bright face-on SABcd type galaxy, which produced several Type II supernovae: SNe 1948B, 1980K, 2002hh, 2004et and 2017eaw. Due to its proximity, the distance of this galaxy could be estimated through the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) method Anand2018 and the planetary nebulae luminosity function (PNLF) relation Herrmann2008 . Three of the aforementioned supernovae (SNe 2002hh, 2004et and 2017eaw) were observed spectroscopically during the early photospheric phase, which makes it possible to measure their distances through the tailored-EPM.

We note that our distance estimates are in much better agreement with one another, which highlights the advantage of the tailored-EPM over the classical EPM approach. The distances are also consistent with the various independent distances obtained for NGC 6946: apart from the previous EPM-based values, our estimates are in agreement with SCM distances of and (both of which yielded D = 6.69 0.30 Mpc for SN 2004et) and the PNLF distance ( Herrmann2008 , D = 6.1 0.6 Mpc), but is slightly lower than the latest TRGB estimate ( D = 6.95 0.20 Mpc, Anand2021 , from the Extragalactic Distance Database https://edd.ifa.hawaii.edu/ ).

sources.summary: The chapter discusses NGC 6946 as the nearby galaxy where magnetic arms were discovered, emphasizing polarized radio emission, stronger interarm magnetic fields, and radial differences from stellar arms.

quotes.quote: A striking evidence of the nontrivial behaviour of the large-scale galactic magnetic field is the so-called magnetic arms, first discovered by Beck Hoernes (1996) in the nearby galaxy NGC 6946. We show in Fig. n6946 a map of polarized radio emission from this galaxy (a tracer of the large-scale magnetic field strength) superimposed on the map in the H spectral line (a tracer of ionized gas). It is evident that magnetic field is stronger between the spiral arms of this galaxy, i.e., where the gas density (both total and ionized) is lower.

Arm intensities in magnetic field and stellar surface density in NGC 6946 have been estimated by Frick \ (2000) who applied wavelet transform techniques to radio polarization maps and to the galaxy image in broadband red light, a tracer of stellar mass density. Their results indicate that the mean relative intensity of magnetic spiral arms remains rather constant with galactocentric radius at a level of 0.3--0.6. On the contrary, the relative strength of the stellar arms systematically grows with radius from very small values in the inner galaxy to 0.3--0.7 at s=5 -6 kpc, and then decreases to remain at a level of 0.1--0.3 out to s=12 . The distinct magnitudes and radial trends in the strengths of magnetic and stellar arms in NGC 6946 do not seem to support the idea that the magnetic arms are due to MHD density waves.

sources.summary: The paper uses L02 HST cluster data for NGC 6946 and argues its luminosity function shows a possible bright-end bend consistent with a maximum cluster mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 5236 and NGC 6946 Data for these two galaxies was taken from L02 and we refer to that paper for details about the data reduction and analysis. Briefly, clusters were identified on V band equivalent (F555W and F606W) HST/WFPC2 images and aperture photometry was carried out using a 3 pixels aperture. Cluster candidates were confirmed by a visual inspection of the images. For NGC 5236 and NGC 6946 there were 1 and 3 HST/WFPC2 pointings available, corresponding to 7.1 kpc ^2 /53 kpc ^2 , respectively.

The LFs of NGC 6946 and M51 are better described by a double power-law distribution or a Schechter function. When a cluster population has a mass function that is truncated below the limit given by the size-of-sample effect, the total LF shows a bend at the magnitude of the maximum mass, with the age of the oldest cluster in the population, typically a few Gyr due to disruption. For NGC 6946 and M51 this suggests a maximum mass of = 0.5-1 10^6 , although the bend is only a 1-2 detection.

sources.summary: The paper presents SCUBA 450 and 850 micron images of NGC 6946, finding sub-millimeter emission associated with molecular gas, spiral arms, star-forming regions, and optically thick dust.

quotes.quote: We present sub-millimetre images of the spiral galaxy NGC 6946 at 450 and 850 m. The observations have been carried out using the scan/map imaging mode of the Sub-millimetre Common User Bolometer Array (SCUBA) at the James Clark Maxwell Telescope (JCMT). The comparison with a ^ 12 CO(2-1) line emission map from IRAM and with an U-band image, clearly shows that the 850 m emission is associated with molecular gas and bright star-forming regions, tracing the spiral arms. We place an upper limit of 0.7 for the V-band optical depth of dust associated with a diffuse atomic component.

The dust content of NGC 6946 has been studied carrying out an energy balance between the stellar emission in the optical and the FIR dust emission, through the help of radiative transfer models. If an exponential disk is used to model the dust distribution, a central face-on optical depth _V 5 is needed to explain the FIR emission (Evans ; Trewhella ; Bianchi et al. ). The 850 m image clearly show that the dust distribution is more complex, but still the column densities derived from the sub-mm flux support the idea of an optically thick dust distribution.

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC 6946's strong spiral-arm magnetic fields, high magnetic and cosmic-ray energy densities, possible outer-disk magnetic dominance, and magnetic arms modeled as shifted azimuthal dynamo modes.

quotes.quote: Radio-faint galaxies like M 31 and M 33, our Milky Way's neighbors, have weaker total magnetic fields (about 5 G), while gas-rich galaxies with high star-formation rates, like M 51 (Fig. fig:m51 ), M 83 and NGC 6946, have total field strengths of 20--30 G in their spiral arms. The degree of radio polarization within the spiral arms is only a few ; hence the field in the spiral arms must be mostly tangled or randomly oriented within the telescope beam, which typically corresponds to a few 100 pc. Turbulent fields in spiral arms are probably generated by turbulent gas motions related to supernovae ( ), stellar winds, spiral shocks ( ) or a small-scale turbulent dynamo ( ). The mean energy densities of the magnetic field and of the cosmic rays in NGC 6946 and M 33 are 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 3 and 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 3 , respectively ( and this volume), about 10 times larger than that of the ionized gas, but similar to that of the turbulent gas motions across the whole star-forming disk. The magnetic energy possibly dominates in the outer disk of NGC 6946.
In some galaxies the field forms magnetic arms \ between the optical arms, like in NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:n6946 ). These are probably generated by a large-scale dynamo.
The magnetic arms in NGC 6946 can be described by a superposition of two azimuthal dynamo modes which are phase shifted with respect to the optical arms ( ).

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 6946 as a gas-rich spiral with strong total fields, magnetic energy comparable to turbulent gas motions, and magnetic arms shaped by dynamo modes.

quotes.quote: Radio-faint galaxies like M 31 (Fig. fig:m31 ) and M 33 taba08 , our Milky Way's neighbors, have weaker total magnetic fields (about 6 G), while gas-rich spiral galaxies with high star-formation rates, like M 51 and NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:m51 ), have total field strengths of 20-30 G in their spiral arms. The mean energy densities of the total (mostly turbulent) magnetic field and the cosmic rays in NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:n6946_energies ) and M 33 are 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 3 and 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 3 , respectively beck07a,taba08 , similar to that of the turbulent gas motions in the star-forming disk, about 10 times larger than that of the ionized gas ( low-beta plasma ). Magnetic fields are dynamically important.

Ordered fields are generally strongest (10--15 G) in the regions between \ the optical spiral arms and oriented parallel to the adjacent spiral arms, in some galaxies forming magnetic arms , like in NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:m51 right), with exceptionally high degrees of polarization (up to 50 ). These are probably generated by a large-scale dynamo (see below). Faraday rotation in the magnetic arms of NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:m51 right) and in other similar galaxies can be described by a superposition of two azimuthal dynamo modes ( m = 0 and m = 2 ) with about equal amplitudes where the quadrisymmetric spiral mode is phase shifted with respect to the density wave beck07a .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as a close analog for NGC 891, citing H I hole masses and counts while arguing its holes cannot supply the observed extraplanar and coronal gas.

quotes.quote: We do not have direct observations in NGC 891 (although dust filaments indicating bubbles and a somewhat porous disk are present), but we can estimate the cold mass from the boomsma08 study of H 1 holes in NGC 6946, a nearby face-on spiral with a similar mass and SFR as NGC 891.
For example, boomsma08 identify 121 holes in NGC 6946 while bagetakos11 find 56 in the same galaxy.

This can also be seen in NGC 6946, whose H 1 holes we used to derive our values. For our fiducial values, the total amount of gas that rises into the halo in NGC 6946 is no more than 15 of the total extraplanar H 1 boomsma08 and coronal gas schlegel03 . Even with optimistic assumptions about the true hole size and amount of kinetic energy involved, it is difficult to envision increasing the outflow mass by an order of magnitude without ejecting the entire swept-up shell mass of each contributing hole, which is impossible for isolated holes. The situation is even worse in NGC 891, which has a similar SFR to NGC 6946 (and therefore presumably about the same number of holes) but four times more extraplanar H 1 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 6946 as a grand-design spiral but finds no significant triggering, noting its flocculent structure and a bias from missing non-arm H II regions.

quotes.quote: In Fig. epsi6946 the relative efficiency as a function of the deprojected radius for NGC 6946 is shown. Star formation triggering does not seem to exist for all the arms of this galaxy, but just a small increase for one of the arms at R=2 kpc. This is not surprising, because: a) there is a lack of interarm H II regions and b) even if this galaxy is classified as class 9, according to Elmegreen Elmegreen (1987), it presents a more chaotic structure of the arms when compared with NGC 628 and NGC 5457, and it can be considered as more flocculent than the other galaxies of the sample (Foyle et al.

The results seem to suggest a absence of triggering of star formation for NGC 6946. However, it should be noted that this galaxy is more flocculent than the other two. On the other hand, NGC 6946 presents a clear lack of H II regions not generated in the arms that may introduce a bias in the calculation of the efficiency.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6946 as a nearby face-on spiral with high star formation, extensive CO data, prominent arms, many supernovae, H II regions, H I holes, and GMCs.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we thus aim to constrain the physical conditions in the star-forming regions of one particular galaxy, NGC 6946, in the hope of futhering our understanding of SF processes. NGC 6946 is an almost face-on, nearby ( 5.5 Mpc; t88 ) spiral galaxy whose basic properties are listed in Table tab:ngc6946 . It has been intensively studied at all wavelengths, making it a particularly good target to study SF processes using a multitude of tracers.

As shown in Figure fig:regions , NGC 6946 has prominent East/North-East spiral arms and more flocculent West/South-West arms a66 , allowing us to probe possible differences in the gas physical properties as a function of radius in two physically different parts of the galaxy. Interestingly, NGC 6946 has hosted 9 recorded supernovae in the last 100 years ( 3 in the past 10 years alone), the highest number in any galaxy s94,f08 . The rate of supernova explosions at the centre of NGC 6946 is as high as 6 10^ 3 yr ^ 1 errl96 .

sources.summary: NGC 6946 is treated as a regular nearby spiral whose residual H I velocity field is well matched by a ram-pressure wind with a 32 km/s effective velocity change.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 is a nearby spiral galaxy at a distance of 5.9 Mpc, exhibiting a very regular stellar (as seen in the near-IR) and atomic gas distribution with no indications of interaction features or gas stripping. An overview of the deprojected stellar distribution, atomic gas distribution and velocity field is shown in Fig. fig:NGC6946dpj which are derived from the high-spatial resolution HI moment-0 and moment-1 maps published in the THINGS survey. We have derived a nominal circular rotation model based on a constant PA and inclination of 243 and 33 , respectively see also deB08 .

The derived model is presented in Fig fig:NGC6946_fig5 , which shows a very good agreement with the observed residual velocity field. An overview of the model parameters is given in Tab. tab:obs_par and Tab. tab:obs_res . As shown in Fig. fig:NGC6946_fig6 our results demonstrate that the dominant residual velocity in NGC 6946 is consistent with a ram pressure wind inducing an effective velocity change of v =32 3 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 6946 with NFW and ISO halos, noting abundant centrally concentrated CO, central HI deficiency, and molecular gas effects on the inner rotation curve and halo parameters.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 In Figures fig:N6946-NFW and fig:N6946-ISO we plot the results of the mass modelling for NGC 6946 for the NFW and ISO halos respectively. For NGC 6946 we use a 2-component stellar decomposition to determine the stellar predicted rotation curves. NGC 6946 has a large amount of CO detected.

Using the Milky Way conversion factor, 2008AJ....136.2782L showed that the molecular gas mass surface density is as high as 400 . This corresponds to a molecular gas predicted rotation curve which reaches a maximum of 60 at approximately 1 . The CO emission shows a compact, bulge like distribution 2008AJ....136.2782L , similar to the stellar component.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as a nearby starburst spiral with an extended HI halo, high star formation, many supernovae, and a peculiar Red Ellipse nebula in its northern arm.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946, a nearby starburst spiral galaxy, also known as the ``Fireworks galaxy'' because of its prolific supernova production, is embedded in an enormous HI halo (Pisano 2014). The galaxy has a high star formation rate and a number of unusual star-forming regions, such as enigmatic Hodge complex which contains a young super star cluster Larsen2002, Efremov2004,Efremov2007 . NGC 6946 is famous also for hosting the record number of observed SNe.

The ellipse is assumed to be within NGC 6946, because it is within the chain of HI clouds, H II regions, and luminous stars comprising the isolated Northern arm of this galaxy (Fig. fig_image ). At a distance to NGC 6946 of 5.9 Mpc according the updated nearby galaxy catalog by Karachentsev2013 , the ellipse has a very large size of approximately 170 290 pc. The accepted distance is close to the mean value of a few dozen measurements of the NGC 6946 distance in the literature, while the most recent values are 5.86 0.76 Mpc BoseKumar2014 and 6.72 0.15 Mpc Tikhonov2014 .

sources.summary: The review describes AME studies of NGC6946 star-forming regions, including significant 33 GHz excess emission and follow-up detections attributed to AME.

quotes.quote: observed 10 star-forming regions in the nearby galaxy NGC6946 at Ka-band (33 GHz) with the GBT. They found evidence for several regions having a marginal excess at 33 GHz, with one region (Enuc 4) being very significant ( 7 ). This region appears to emit 50 AME at 33 GHz, which was confirmed by follow-up observations with the AMI telescope at 15--18 GHz Scaife2010a . A spinning dust model for the spectrum was preferred over the free-free-only model.

Follow-up observations of NGC 6946 with CARMA, at yet higher angular resolution (21 arcsec), revealed additional regions having excess 33 GHz emission attributed to AME see Fig. fig:bms15 Hensley2015 . The strength of the excess emission in these regions was found to be correlated with the total Infrared (IR; 8-- 1000 m) luminosity, lending credence to the interpretation as AME.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 577 star-cluster/H II region pairs in NGC 6946, noting its regular morphology, low Galactic latitude, reliable statistics, and propagation slopes.

quotes.quote: The final sample includes 503 SC-H ii R pairs in NGC 628, 70 pairs in NGC 3184, 254 pairs in NGC 3726, 40 pairs in NGC 5585, and 577 pairs in NGC 6946.
A special case is NGC 6946 which is located on the low Galactic latitude (see column (10) in Table table:sample ).

Note that three of five studied galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 3184, and NGC 6946) have a regular symmetrical morphology. NGC 5585 has rather the Large Magellanic Cloud type. NGC 3726 has a distorted asymmetric disc. In addition, it is the most distant galaxy of the sample and, therefore, we were unable to identify a sufficient number of close SC-H ii R pairs with S<80 pc in it (Fig. figure:age_sep ). The most reliable results are those obtained for NGC 628 and NGC 6946. On the one hand, these galaxies have a symmetrical regular structure, on the other hand, they provide us with a large statistical material for analysis -- more than 500 SC-H ii R pairs. The graphs in Fig. figure:age_sep and the coefficients given in Table table:coeff show closely resembling slopes for cluster samples in three galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 3184, and NGC 6946) with a regular structure.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 6946 as a four-component disc, infers a nearly constant thick molecular gas fraction, and estimates substantial extra-planar molecular gas.

quotes.quote: Assuming a two-component molecular disc, we model the disc of NGC 6946 as a four-component system consisting of stars, , thin disc molecular gas, and thick disc molecular gas in vertical hydrostatic equilibrium.

We find that the thick disc molecular gas fraction in NGC 6946 largely remains constant across its molecular disc with a mean value of 0.70 0.09 . We also estimate the amount of extra-planar molecular gas in NGC 6946. We find 50 of the total molecular gas is extra-planar at the central region, whereas this fraction reduces to 15 at the edge of the molecular disc.

In conclusion, we model the molecular disc in NGC 6946 as a two-component system with a thin disc having a low velocity dispersion of 7  and a thick disc with a velocity dispersion equal to that of the . With this, we model the baryonic disc in NGC 6946 as a four-component system consisting of a stellar disc,  disc, and two molecular discs (thin and thick).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as an isolated nearby face-on spiral with a type-9 arm structure, many core-collapse supernovae, outer-disk H emission, and strong star formation extending into its extreme outer disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 NGC 6946 is a nearby face-on galaxy at 5.9 Mpc distance with an inclination angle of 33 ^ . Its spiral arm structure is classified as type 9 by Elmegreen1984 . This galaxy is also known as the Firework Galaxy as ten core-collapse supernovae have been detected in its disk since 1917 Eldridge2019 . It is also one of the few galaxies in the local universe that shows H emission in its outer disk Ferguson1998 .

The galaxy is isolated and located in the interior of a nearby void Tully1988, Sharina1997 . However, it still shows a very large amount of star formation that extends well into its extreme outer disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as its detailed example, checks the best-fit model with RADEX, and derives CO and HCN conversion profiles with CO-to-H2 increasing outward.

quotes.quote: In this section we focus on the results of NGC 6946 as an example; the results for the other galaxies are available in the Appendix appendix .

As a consistency check we used RADEX for the Q grid calculations for NGC 6946 with the best-fit values of , , _0 , and given in Table bestfit1 . The resulting RADEX best-fit model yields a better fit to the CO(1-0) and CO(2-1) line emission, a less good fit to the SFR in the inner disk, a lower Q parameter ( Q =1 ), a comparable , and a somewhat lower gas velocity dispersion compared to the model where RADEX was used a posteriori. Since these changes are within the uncertainties of the model, we are confident that our derived model parameters are meaningful.

We computed the CO and HCN conversion factor profiles of NGC 6946, ngc6946b (g and h). For both ^2 calculations, the CO-to-H _2 conversion factors increase from the galaxy center to the edge of the CO emission distribution, with a mean close to the Galactic value of = 4.36 ( 2013ARA&A..51..207B ).

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC 6946 as a spiral in a low-density region whose normal spiral appearance, extended H I, and satellite retinue challenge simple environment-based intuition.

quotes.quote: Another interesting object in the low density region toward the top of Figure fig:LocalVol is the spiral galaxy NGC 6946. It is marked by the open red circle at the largest positive value of SGZ. The ambient density is low there, but the image of this galaxy (to be seen on the web) looks much like the large spirals in the far more crowed region near the plane of the Local Supercluster that runs across the middle of the figure. A quantitative measure of that is the tight relation between the spiral galaxy circular velocity and baryonic mass (McGaugh 2020), which does not offer much room for sensitivity to environment. The atomic hydrogen surrounding NGC 6946 extends well beyond the starlight (Boomsma, Oosterloo, Fraternali, van der Hulst, and Sancisi, 2008), and it has the customary retinue of dwarf satellite galaxies (Karachentsev, Sharina, and Huchtmeier 2000).
The galaxy NGC 6946 is a counterexample to one of those arguments that seem to make intuitive sense.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 6946 through thousands of local star-forming sub-regions, using extinction, stellar and gas surface densities, and Monte Carlo dust coupling to estimate radiation-pressure Eddington ratios.

quotes.quote: Here we assess the role of radiation pressure in thousands of sub-regions across two local star-forming galaxies, NGC 6946 and NGC 5194. Using a model for the spectral energy distribution from stellar population synthesis and realistic dust grain scattering and absorption, we compute flux- and radiation pressure-mean opacities and population-averaged optical depth _ RP . Using Monte-Carlo calculations, we assess the momentum coupling through a dusty column to the stellar continuum.

For the individual sub-regions in NGC 5194 and 6946, we use the observed values of L_ H , the extinction A_ H , the total stellar surface density projected for each region, and the average local projected HI and molecular gas surface densities to calculate the Eddington ratio (data from Kessler2020 ). Figures figure:Edd_hist and figure:Edd_hist_planar show the distribution of Eddington ratios in sub-regions for different values of the age of the sub-regions and the height of the dusty shell. Tables table:integralQuantities and table:integralQuantitiesPlanar show the fraction of the bolometric luminosity and masses in all studied sub-regions that are super-Eddington.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6946 among four barred spirals with inner and outer CO-to-H2 conversion-factor and DGR measurements, separating center/bar regions from spiral arms and finding higher outer conversion factors and lower outer DGR.

quotes.quote: Finally, we obtained \ and DGR in the inner and outer regions of the following four barred spiral galaxies: IC 342, NGC 3627, NGC 5236, and NGC 6946, as listed in table tab:alpha_DGR_radial . The boundary of 0.2 R_ 25 in each four galaxies is shown in figure fig:R25_Vband . The inclination and the position angle were corrected.

In these galaxies, the inner region includes the center and the bar structure, while the outer region includes the spiral arms. The average and standard deviation of (inner) and DGR(inner) are 0.36 0.08 \ and 0.0199 0.0058 , while (outer) and DGR(outer) are 1.49 0.76 \ and 0.0084 0.0037 , respectively. In each galaxy, while (outer) is 2.3 to 5.3 times larger than (inner), DGR(outer) is 0.3 to 0.6 times smaller than that DGR(inner).

sources.summary: The paper presents N2H+ and HCN observations across NGC 6946, a gas-rich double-barred spiral, finding correlated intensities and a nearly constant dense-gas line ratio across its disk.

quotes.quote: We provide the first systematic measurements of N _2 H ^+ (1-0) across different environments of an external spiral galaxy, NGC 6946. We find a strong correlation ( >0.98 ,p<0.01 ) between the HCN (1-0) and N _2 H ^+ (1-0) intensities across the inner 8 kpc of the galaxy, at kiloparsec scales.

We present the first extragalactic observations of N _2 H ^+ (1-0) and HCN (1-0) in a wide range of dynamical conditions and star formation properties sampled across an entire galaxy disk, NGC 6946. This nearby source 7.72 Mpc, Anand2018Distance is a gas-rich, very actively star-forming double-barred spiral galaxy.

We measure I_ N _2 H ^+ /I_ HCN ratios that range between 0.12 and 0.20 in NGC 6946, with a mean value of 0.15 0.02 and a standard deviation of 0.03. Our line ratios agree well with the range of observed I_ N _2 H ^+ /I_ HCN values in nearby starbursts, AGNs, and ULIRGs (see also Fig. fig:literature ), as reflected by the purple area in the plot. This compilation is available in Table tab:lit_data . The middle panel of Fig. fig:observed_ratios shows the HCN (1-0)-to-CO (1-0) intensity ratios ( I_ HCN /I_ CO ) from EMPIRE Donaire2019 extracted at the same positions. While I_ HCN /I_ CO systematically decreases by a factor of 4 with increasing galactocentric distance, I_ N _2 H ^+ /I_ HCN appears to be constant across the galaxy disk.

sources.summary: The paper measures column-density and velocity power spectra in NGC 6946, finding a kiloparsec-scale forward turbulence cascade with mixed solenoidal and compressive driving.

quotes.quote: In this work, we explore the nature of large-scale turbulence in the external spiral galaxy NGC 6946. We use the Visibility Moment Estimator (VME) to measure the column density and line of sight turbulent velocity power spectra combining the new observations of A array configuration of Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) with the VLA B, C, D array observations from The Nearby Galaxy Survey (THINGS). The estimated power spectra are obeying a power law with a slope of 0.96 0.05 in column density and 1.81 0.07 in line of sight velocity in length scales ranging from 6 kpc to 170 pc. This points towards a forward energy cascade in the plane of the disc with a driving scale at least as large as 6 kpc.

NGC 6946 is a SABcd-type bright late-type spiral galaxy with prominent spiral structures 1991rc3..book.....D,2014MNRAS.444L..85F . Using the luminosity-based estimate by 2004AJ....127.2031K we adopt a distance of 5.9 Mpc to the galaxy in our analysis.

sources.summary: The paper models far-infrared dust emission in NGC 6946, finding optically thick dust disks are needed while the observed 200 m scalelength remains unreproduced.

quotes.quote: The model optical and FIR scalelengths and the SED have been compared to NGC 6946 observations. Several models have been explored. It is found that optically thick dust disks ( _ V 5) are needed to match the observed FIR output. Approximately one third of the total stellar radiation is absorbed by dust in this case.

AltonA&A1998 found that the B-band scalelength of NGC 6946 is smaller than the 200 m one by a factor 0.9 (0.8 for a sample of seven galaxies). We have not been able to reproduce the large FIR scalelengths measured on 200 m ISO data.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as a precise magnetic-field test case, arguing that its slowly declining field profile can provide a large inward force relevant to flat spiral-galaxy rotation curves.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC6946, for which very precise measurements are available (Beck 2004a), magnetic energy densities are 10^ 11 erg cm^ 3 at 4 kpc (see fig. 1). These strengths are of the order of magnitude needed for magnetic forces to become important and even dominant at very large radii.

Fig. 1 plots the magnetic energy density as deduced from observations in NGC 6946 (Beck 2004b) together with the critical R^ 1 profile passing through the point at 4 kpc. It is evident that the B_ profile is sub-critical for R 3 kpc, i.e. the net magnetic force is inwards. Following Beck, the field energy density has a radial scale length that is much larger than the neutral density scale length of only 3 kpc. The slow decrease of B_ with R is able to produce a very large centripetal force.

sources.summary: The paper resolves dense molecular gas in NGC 6946’s central 50 pc, interpreting it as bar-driven gas accumulation linked to embedded star formation and central mass build-up.

quotes.quote: We have used the new extended A configuration of the IRAM Plateau de Bure interferometer to study the dense molecular gas in the nucleus of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 6946 at unprecedented spatial resolution in the HCN(1-0) and \ lines. The gas distribution in the central 50 pc has been resolved and is consistent with a gas ring or spiral driven by the inner 400 pc long stellar bar. For the first time, it is possible to directly compare the location of (dense) giant molecular clouds with that of (optically) visible HII regions in space-based images. We use the 3 mm continuum and the HCN emission to estimate in the central 50 pc the star formation rates in young clusters that are still embedded in their parent clouds and hence are missed in optical and near-IR surveys of star formation.

The amount of embedded star formation is about 1.6 times as high as that measured from HII regions alone, and appears roughly evenly split between ongoing dust-obscured star formation and very young giant molecular cloud cores that are just beginning to form stars. The build-up of central mass seems to have continued over the past 10 Myrs, to have occurred in an extended (albeit small) volume around the nucleus, and to be closely related to the presence of an inner bar.

sources.summary: The paper compares its magnetic-reconnection heating model with NGC 6946, linking predicted X-ray luminosity to ROSAT diffuse emission that is not explained by unresolved point sources.

quotes.quote: In the expression ( Q_total ) we estimated the total heat output from reconnection in the volume of galactic disc, which typically should be of the order of 10^ 38 10^ 39 / for galaxies similar to ours or NGC 6946.

This gives an upper limit of for the reconnection heated X-ray emiting gas _ rec 0.1 Finally we would suggest that if t_ heat is shorter than t_ cool , a majority of released heat is converted to the X-ray luminosity, which according to our estimations should be 10^ 38 10^ 39 / for galaxies like ours or NGC 6946. Schlegel (1994) analyzes the X-ray ROSAT observations of NGC 6946. The X-ray image of that galaxy is composed of a number of point sources and a diffuse emission. Schlegel concludes that the diffuse emission is not the sum of unresolved weak point sources like supernova remnants and X-ray binaries. We suggest that the unresolved X-ray emission can be related to magnetic heating process described above.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as a low-latitude, well-studied galaxy with about 100 identified clusters, including a striking young luminous cluster in a spiral-arm bubble.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 The study of NGC 6946 is complicated by the fact that it is located at low galactic latitude ( b = 12^ ), and there is an interstellar absorption of A_B = 1.6 magnitudes and a large number of field stars towards this galaxy. NGC 6946 is nevertheless a well-studied galaxy, and we also chose to include it in our sample, reasoning that star clusters should be recognizable as extended objects on the NOT data. The chemical abundances of HII regions in NGC 6946 were studied by Ferguson et. al. (1998), who concluded that their data were consistent with a single log-linear dependence on the radius. At 1.5-2 optical radii (defined by the B-band 25th magnitude isophote) they measured abundances of O/H of about 10 15 of the solar value, and N/O of about 20 25 of the solar value. Among the approximately 100 clusters we have identified in NGC 6946, one stands out as particularly striking (Fig. fig:n6946 ).
This cluster is apparently a very young object, located in one of the spiral arms at a distance of 4.4 Kpc from the centre, and with an impressive visual luminosity of M_V = -13 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as the host-galaxy context for SN 2004et, describing it as a starburst spiral, adopting a 5.5 Mpc distance, and comparing its known radio supernovae.

quotes.quote: Supernova SN 2004et in galaxy NGC 6946 was discovered by Zwitter et al. ( ) on 27 September 2004. Yamaoka et al. ( ) reported data from the robotic T elescope a Action Rapide pour les Objects Transitoires (TAROT) telescope from which the supernova explosion date could be well constrained. Nothing brighter than magnitude 19.4 1.2 was seen on September 22.017 and the supernova was detected at magnitude 15.17 0.16 on September 22.983. Following Yamaoka et al., we assume 22.0 September 2004 as the explosion date. NGC 6946 is a starburst spiral galaxy with a high supernova production rate. In fact, SN 2004et is the eighth supernova event registered in this galaxy since 1917. The distance estimates to NGC 6946 range from 5.1 Mpc (de Vaucouleurs ) up to 10.1 Mpc (Sandage Tammann ). Here we adopt 5.5 0.1 Mpc as the distance to NGC 6946 following Pierce ( ), who estimated it using the Tully-Fisher HI relation.

Comparison with other supernovae in NGC 6946 From those eight supernovae known to have exploded in NGC 6946, only four have been detected as radio supernovae: SN 1968D , SN 1980K , SN 2002hh , and SN2004 et.

sources.summary: The paper detects CO emission in the far outer disk of NGC 6946, deriving molecular column densities and masses and finding a sharp decline in molecular fraction near the optical edge.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a search for molecular gas emission via the CO line in the far outer disk of the nearby spiral, NGC 6946. The positions targeted were chosen to lie on or near previously-identified outer disk HII regions. Molecular gas was clearly detected out to 1.3 R _ 25 , with a further tentative detection at 1.4 R _ 25 . The CO detections show excellent agreement with the HI velocities and imply beam-averaged column densities of 0.3-9 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 and molecular gas masses of (2-70) 10^ 5 M _ per 21 '' beam (560pc). We find evidence for an abrupt decrease in the molecular fraction at the edge of the optical disk, similar to that seen previously in the azimuthally-averaged areal star formation rate.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 6946 as a nearby spiral with anomalous H I, disk holes, widespread high-velocity gas, and halo gas linked to star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 is a bright, nearby spiral galaxy of Hubble type Scd (inclination angle 38 ^ ) which has been studied in \ several times . It was in this galaxy that Kamphuis and Sancisi (1993) found evidence for an anomalous-velocity \ component which they interpreted as an outflow of gas from the disk into the halo as a result of stellar winds and supernova explosions. A more recent, very sensitive and detailed study of the anomalous \ and of the \ disk has been carried out by Boomsma (2007) with the WSRT. The density distribution in the \ disk is characterized by the presence of a large number of holes of diameters up to 2 kpc. The average amount of \ missing from each hole is about 1 10^7 , the total amount is 1.1 10^9 .

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 6946, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:ngc-6946 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] NGC 6946 is a late-type spiral galaxy with well-defined multiple spiral arms. The IRAC 3.6 m image shows a point source in the center of the galaxy which coincides to better than 1 \ with the central radio continuum source. We use the position of the latter ( =-1 8 , =14 5 ) as input for a \ fit with all parameters left free and determine the dynamical center by averaging the , \ values for 22 290 , thus excluding the two innermost points which are affected by sparsely filled tilted-rings. The resulting dynamical center ( =-3 3 4 4 , =11 8 5 8 ) coincides within the uncertainties and to within one beam with the other center estimates (see Fig.

sources.summary: The paper strongly detects polarized emission in NGC 6946's northeast half, with low-to-high radial polarization, optical-arm-aligned magnetic fields, and interarm polarized peaks.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 The polarized emission is strongly detected from the northeast half of this galaxy, tapering away toward the southwest. The minimum in polarized intensity occurs at the PA of the receding major axis (PA = 243 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). The polarized fraction (in the northeast) is quite low in the inner parts at less than 10 , moderate at intermediate radii at about 20--30 , and very high in the outer parts, reaching up to (and perhaps above) 40--50 . The magnetic field lines are closely related to the large-scale spiral morphology traced by massive star formation and dust lanes, running largely parallel to the optical arms. As has been pointed out by beck_2007 , the peaks in polarized emission originate in the interarm regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as an isolated nearby spiral edging into the Local Void, with a near-pure stellar disk and no bulge despite standard-looking angular momentum.

quotes.quote: M 101 and NGC 6946 are above plane, edging into the Local Void. NGC 6946 is the most isolated, with only a few small neighbors. Kormendy and Fisher note that NGC 6946 is a near pure disc of stars, without the stellar bulge expected to result from mergers or close interactions with large neighbors after a disk of stars first formed. That seems consistent with its isolation.

NGC 6946 does not have the bulge one associates with disturbances by massive neighbors after first disk formation, which seems consistent with its near isolation, but it is curious that it nevertheless was torqued up to a standard-looking angular momentum.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6946 as a disk-field example whose Faraday-depth pattern depends on the adopted foreground value and has asymmetric extrema, with faint secondary polarized components offset by about 200 rad m^-2.

quotes.quote: Classic examples of disk-dominated fields can be seen in galaxies such as NGC 628, 5194, and 6946, although they are present in almost all of our targets with varying detectability.

This same pattern applies to many of the eight lowest inclination galaxies and would also apply to NGC 6946 if the previously published estimate of the Galactic foreground value, _ FG = 40 by beck_2007 , were the correct one, rather than the _ FG = 23 2 we estimate in Paper II. A similar consideration applies to NGC 5194, for which horellou_etal_1992 estimated _ FG = 5 12 rather than our estimate of _ FG = +12 2 . We plotted these alternate estimates of _ FG in the figure with horizontal lines. We also note here a typographical error in the value of _ FG for NGC 4321 in Table 1 of Paper II, which is 7 and not 17 as stated there. This pattern of maximum and minimum is not a simple sinusoid but has a clear asymmetry about an azimuth of 180 ^ that is most obvious in NGC 4321 and 6946.

Solid detections of faint secondary emission components were made in the brightest face-on galaxies of our sample, as shown in Figs. figure:n628smo - figure:n6946smo . In addition to the bright polarized emission that typically resides at a Faraday depth within only a few tens of rad m ^ 2 of the Galactic foreground value, much fainter polarized emission (by a factor of 4 -- 5) is detected in NGC 628, 5194, and 6946, which is offset to both positive and negative Faraday depths by about 200 rad m ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as a nearby spiral with magnetic arms and coherent large-scale field structure, large disk scale radius, isolation, and no signs of major tidal distortion.

quotes.quote: Among the existing observations, we could find spiral galaxies with well-devel oped large-scale spiral (axisymmetric) magnetic field structures, like in M 31 beck82, berkh03 , IC 342 krause89 and NGC 6946 beck07 , as visible in polarized intensity and Faraday rotation similar to the last row in Fig. fig:sim5ghz . All three galaxies are large with an exponential scale radius of about 5 kpc. Hence, according to it should have taken about 9 Gyr for these galaxies to coherently order the regular field structure over the entire disk (twice scale radius). This indicates that they did not suffer any major merger during this period. Indeed, NGC 6946 and IC 342 are isolated galaxies without any known companion and without any signs of major tidal distortions.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 6946 as a nearby spiral whose magnetic arms lie between material arms, motivating dynamo models with finite relaxation-time phase shifts.

quotes.quote: In most cases, there is clearly a relationship between the material spiral arms and regular magnetic field spiral arms. For instance, there exists a correspondence between the azimuthally averaged pitch angle of the regular field and that of the material spiral f10 . This would be of little surprise, as the material arms naturally leave their imprint on the magnetic field, if not for the intriguing relation between magnetic and material arms, first discovered in the nearby galaxy NGC 6946 behoernes96 . Here the magnetic arms are located almost precisely between the material arms. They appear to be phase-shifted images of the material arms, with a negative phase shift in the sense of the galactic rotation fricketal00, be07 .

sources.summary: The paper uses OSIRIS tunable-filter observations of NGC 6946 to demonstrate 2D spectroscopy of HII regions, including an H+[NII] scan, sky subtraction, pseudo-spectra, and profile deconvolution.

quotes.quote: Another example of the OSIRIS scientific advantages is the 2D spectroscopy of extended sources, i.e., the HII regions of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 6946 (Cedr es et al. in preparation). Figure fig2 (left) shows the synthesis of an H + [ NII ] RTF scan of this galaxy. The successful strategy of investing observing time in the outfield sampling of airglow emission lines, allowed a very accurate subtraction of these features. Subsequent analysis of the scan slices provided pseudo-spectra of the isolated HII regions. The Figure fig2 (right) depicts the observed pseudo-spectrum of a selected HII region in NGC 6946 and its deconvolution of the instrumental profile.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as an observed extreme where magnetic and optical arms share pitch angles, show measured phase shifts, and may require complex spiral structure.

quotes.quote: One extreme is the galaxy NGC 6946 where magnetic arms appear to have the same pitch angle as the optical arms, and the two patterns do not intersect. The azimuthal phase shifts between the individual optical arms and their magnetic counterparts identified by fricketal00 are 26^ 12^ , 36^ 11^ and 45^ 17^ at 4 and, for an outer arm, 68^ 17^ at 8 , where negative phase shift corresponds to a position behind a gaseous arm all distances have been reduced to a distance of 5.5 to NGC 6946 -- Ketal03 . According to the H observations and analysis of FTTBHDCZ07 , the co-rotation radius of the outer spiral pattern in this galaxy is at about 8.3 , whereas the interlaced magnetic and optical spiral arms occur at 1 9 .

Another possibility is that the spiral structure in NGC 6946 is more complex than that of a single, rigidly rotating pattern. For instance, the arms may be winding up to some extent, and thus transient e.g. dobbsetal10, sellwood11, quillenetal11, wadaetal11, grandetal12 .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 6946 magnetic fields with prior work, measuring magnetic arms around 11 G and a low-field circular hole about 30 percent below nearby regions.

quotes.quote: Magnetic field in NGC 6946 was studied in detail by beck07 . The total magnetic field strength was found to be 20 G in the spiral gas arms, close to what is estimated by us. In the arms turbulent fields dominate, while in the interarms large scale regular field was seen with high degree of polarization (30--60 ) referred as the ``magnetic arms'' by beck07 . From our maps (see Fig. bmaps ), in the northern (roughly centered at RA = 20^h34^m52^s , DEC = +60^ 11^ 59 J2000) and southern (roughly centered at RA = 20^h34^m33^s , DEC = +60^ 06^ 46.75 J2000) magnetic arms, the average field is found to be 11 G, which is just 10--15 stronger than other interarm regions, and this result is at 3 significance. The ``circular hole'' of 1 kpc diameter seen in the galaxy with low radio emission at RA = 20^h34^m20^s and Dec. = +60^ 09^ 40 (J2000) is seen to have low magnetic field ( 8.5 G) as compared to other parts and is 30 lower than the surrounding regions.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as a key observational example where massive spiral arms lack density-wave shocks and prominent magnetic arms lie between material arms.

quotes.quote: The most spectacular example is provided by NGC 6946, a galaxy with massive spiral arms, but without density-wave shock fronts. The magnetic arms are clearly situated between material arms (Beck Hoernes 1996, Beck 2007). The magnetic arms in NGC 6946 are most prominent in polarized emission. Strong Faraday rotation in the magnetic arms shows that the field is large-scale (regular). On the other hand, the random magnetic field (traced by the unpolarized emission) is strongest in the material arms.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as an observed spiral-galaxy benchmark with two- and three-arm patterns, interarm magnetic arms, and near-perfect magnetic/optical arm alignment.

quotes.quote: Many of these results are reminiscent of observed features in the regular magnetic fields of nearby spiral galaxies, like NGC 6946, which has previously been inferred to have significant two- and three-arm spiral patterns, and IC 342, which has been reported to contain an inner two-arm and outer four-arm pattern.
However, extreme cases with |p_B| as large as 40^ as in the galaxy M33 and near-perfect alignment of magnetic and optical arms as in NGC 6946 are clearly not possible to explain using such models.

Some of the features found in the solutions are also found in the galaxy NGC 6946, which is thought to contain an n=2 and an n=3 pattern Elmegreen+92 , and is famous for harbouring magnetic arms that are localized in the interarm regions Beck07 . This galaxy is inferred to have five magnetic arms, with some extending radially for 10 and others located only in the inner or outer regions Frick+00 . This is reminiscent of the complex morphology seen in Model A23 or X23, including magnetic `armlets' that are shorter than the magnetic arms and localized to the outer regions of the galaxy, as well as bifurcations of magnetic arms.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 6946 has two NH3 velocity components, with the +80 km/s component warmer and molecular emission in nuclear structures.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946: report detections of the H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 and 2_ 11 2_ 12 transitions in absorption toward this galaxy, where a two-velocity component structure was noted with v_ hel 8 and +80 . Our measurements of the NH _3 (1,1) and (2,2) transitions are consistent with this two-velocity component structure. The quality of the kinetic temperature fits to both velocity components for this galaxy are good, well constrained by the NH _3 (1,1) and (2,2) transitions detected, suggesting that the +80 \ component (NGC 6946 G2) is significantly warmer than the 8 \ component (NGC 6946 G1; see Figure fig:NGC6946TkFit ). We are not aware of any previous measurements of NH _3 toward this galaxy. High spatial resolution measurements of dense gas tracers in NGC 6946 see summary in Mangum2013 suggest that the molecular emission emanates from two compact components: a compact nuclear source of size 2^ and a ``nuclear spiral'' which is 5^ 10^ in extent.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6946 as the normal-galaxy target in its LMT 3 mm survey and places its dense-gas line ratios near normal galaxies.

quotes.quote: Our selected galaxies, have been detected previously in HCN, and have different degrees of nuclear activity --- one normal galaxy (NGC 6946), the starburst prototype (M 82) and two ultraluminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs, IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231). We plotted our data in the HCO ^+ /HCN vs. HCN/ ^ 13 CO diagnostic diagram finding that NGC 6946 and M 82 are located close to other normal galaxies; and that both IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231 are close to the position of the well known ULIRG Arp 220 reported by  Snell2011 . We found that in Mrk 231 -- a galaxy with a well known active galactic nucleus -- the HCO ^+ /HCN ratio is similar to the ratio observed in other normal galaxies.

The observations discussed in this paper include M 82 which was observed with the RSR at the LMT during the first-light scientific demonstration observations phase in June 2011, and NGC 6946, IRAS 17208-0014 and Mrk 231 that where observed during the summer of 2013. In the observed 3 mm spectra of these galaxies we detected several lines including, in some cases, lines as weak as CN which traces very dense molecular gas (n(H _2) 10^6 cm ^ 3 ). However, we are reporting only our results on the HCN, HCO ^+ and ^ 13 CO lines.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 6946 with IC 342, noting lower spiral-arm polarization, higher magnetic-arm polarization, more ordered magnetic arms, and a much stronger regular ASS field.

quotes.quote: These results indicate that the magnetic field structure in the spiral arms of IC 342 is different from that in NGC 6946 beck07 , where the typical degrees of polarization are lower in the spiral arms (between 5 and 10 ) but higher in the magnetic arms (35 on average, up to 45 locally). In IC 342 the field is less turbulent in the spiral arms than in those of NGC 6946. The northern magnetic arm of IC 342 is shorter and its field is less ordered (more turbulent) than in that of NGC 6946 (see Sect. sect:ma ).

The inclination-corrected amplitude of the ASS field of RM_0 = RM_ max / tan(i) is 13 and 11 in the two rings of IC 342, which is several times smaller than in M 31 fletcher04 and M 51 fletcher11 and about ten times smaller than in NGC 6946 ehle93 .

Assuming an average electron density along the line of sight of <n_ e > 0.03 and a scale height of the ionized gas of h 1000 pc, B_0 0.5 and 0.4 , similar to the values for M 33, but much lower than in NGC 6946 ehle93 and several other spiral galaxies vaneck15 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6946 outer-disk regions with deep CO data, discusses its uncertain XUV-disk classification, and uses its XCO profile in star-formation calculations.

quotes.quote: We also included six regions in the XUV disk of NGC 6946 D = 5.9 Mpc ; schruba11 , six regions in the outer disk of M33 D = 0.96 Mpc ; bonanos06 , and three regions in the XUV disk of NGC 4414 D = 17.7 Mpc ; freedman01 , all of which have deep CO measurements or upper limits braine04, braine07, braine10 . We refer to the outer disk of NGC 6946 as an XUV disk because the galaxy contains UV emission beyond the optical radius. However, this classification is not as clear as for NGC 4625. Specifically, holwerda12 classified NGC 6946 as not having an XUV disk.
The radial profiles of X_ CO in NGC 4625 and NGC 6946 extend to 2.5 r_ 25 and 1.2 r_ 25 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 6946 has a flatter nonthermal spectrum and maps its magnetic-field strength, finding a star-formation correlation driven by turbulent fields.

quotes.quote: Separating the thermal and nonthermal emission in other nearby galaxy with higher SFR, NGC 6946, a flatter spectrum is obtained ( _n 0.8 , Tabatabaei et al. 2013).

The TRT separation technique has allowed mapping the magnetic field strength in M33 (Tabatabaei et al. 2008) and NGC 6946 (Tabatabaei et al. 2013). In both galaxies, the magnetic field strength is correlated with the SFR surface density (Fig. 2). This correlation is more likely due to the turbulent component of the magnetic field, as the SFR surface density has a tight correlation with the turbulent field. This shows an enhancement of the turbulent magnetic field due to star formation feedback, such as supernova and strong shocks.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as a key instability example, then identifies its inner disc as an exception where high molecular gas and SFR make molecular gas, rather than stars, drive instabilities.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 provides an eloquent example of how radically can affect the onset of gravitational instabilities in galaxy discs, and how drastically is approximated in stability analyses (Ferguson et al. \ 1998; Romeo Fathi 2015). In particular, Ferguson et al. \ (1998) showed that if one assumes =6 km s ^ 1 , the classical value motivated by Kennicutt (1989), then this galaxy turns out to be unstable up to the edge of the optical disc, while using a radial profile of derived from observations yields stability across the entire discMartin Kennicutt (2001) pointed out that radial variation in remains controversial because such measurements demand both high angular resolution and high brightness sensitivity, requirements not met by most observations.

Inner disc instabilities are now entirely driven by the stars at kpc scales ( _ 3 =5.9 2.3 kpc ), with two notable exceptions: the inner discs of NGC 5194 and NGC 6946 (2 of the data). These are the sample galaxies with highest surface density of molecular gas averaged over the inner disc ( _ CO 150 M _ pc ^ 2 for R 0.3 R_ 25 ), and highest star formation rate ( SFR 3 M _ yr ^ 1 ; see table 4 of L08, or table 1 of Walter et al. \ 2008). These are also active galaxies, where molecular gas plays a key role as fuel in the activity process (e.g., Krips et al. \ 2008).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6946 as the host of SN 2017eaw, gives the supernova's offset from the galaxy center, and adopts host-direction extinction and distance values.

quotes.quote: We present UBVRI photometry of the supernova 2017eaw in NGC 6946, obtained in the period from May 14 until December 7, 2017.
The SN was located at =20^ h 34^ m 44^ s .238, =+60^ 11'36''.00 , 61''.0 west and 143''.0 north of the center of NGC 6946 (Sarneczky et al., 2017)

The Galactic extinction in the direction of NGC 6946 is E(B-V)=0.30 mag according to Schlafly, Finkbeiner (2011) (via NED ). http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu We suppose, that the extinction for both SNe has nearly equal value and occurs only in the Galaxy. The difference of estimates based on the equivalent width of Na I lines may be due to the large dispersion of results obtained with this method. This supposition is supported by the fact that both SNe exploded at quite large distances from the center of the galaxy: 8 kpc for SN 2004et, 4.7 kpc for SN 2017eaw, in the regions with low surface brightness. Assuming the distance of 6.0 Mpc for NGC 6946 (Efremov et al., 2011) and reddening E(B-V)=0.30 mag we derive absolute magnitudes of SN 2017eaw at maximum light: M_U=-17.9, M_B=-16.9, M_V=-17.0, M_R=-17.1, M_I=-17.2 mag.

sources.summary: The paper compares TRGB and supernova-based distances for NGC 6946, finding SN 2004et, SN 1980K, and SN 2002hh give consistent PMM distances that conflict with TRGB estimates.

quotes.quote: The distance to NGC 6946, host of SN 2004et, was measured with the TRGB method by Tikhonov2014 ( Tikhonov2014 , hereafter T14), Murphy_etal2018 ( Murphy_etal2018 , hereafter M18), and Anand_etal2018_NGC6946_TRGB_distance ( Anand_etal2018_NGC6946_TRGB_distance , hereafter A18), and correspond to =29.39 0.14 mag in the Jang_Lee2017_TRGB_ZP calibration. The PMM J band distance for SN 2004et obtained in this work ( _J=28.83 0.12 mag) is in conflict with the TRGB estimation. To investigate the reason for this discrepancy, we compute distances to other two SNe II that exploded in NGC 6946: SN 1980K and SN 2002hh. These SNe have near-IR photometry, but we did not include them into the analysis because they do not have photometry in the five bands we use (i.e., ). Using data given in Table  table:SN1980K_SN2002hh_quantitites , we obtain _J=28.73 0.18 and 28.77 0.11 mag for SN 1980K and SN 2002hh, respectively, which are consistent with the distance computed with SN 2004et.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6946 as the nearby face-on spiral host of SN 2017eaw and derives an improved 6.85 Mpc distance using SN 2017eaw and 2004et data.

quotes.quote: The combined data of SNe 2017eaw and 2004et allow us to get an improved distance to the host galaxy, NGC 6946, as D 6.85 0.63 Mpc; this fits in recent independent results on the distance of the host and disfavors the previously derived (30 shorter) distances based on SN 2004et.

SN 2017eaw appeared in NGC 6946, at 61.0 '' west and 143.0 '' north from the center of the galaxy. The host is a nearby, face-on spiral galaxy, which has produced around a dozen known SNe and other luminous transients including the Type II-L SN 1980K, Type II-P SNe 1948B, 2002hh and 2004et, and the SN impostor 2008S.

We derived a new distance to the host galaxy, NGC 6946, after combining various distance estimates including the EPM analysis of the combined data of SNe 2017eaw and 2004et. The final distance, D 6.85 0.63 Mpc, disfavors the previous measurements from SN 2004et only that all gave 30 percent lower distances.

sources.summary: EMPIRE describes NGC 6946 as a flocculent, strongly barred nuclear-burst spiral with the brightest HCN emission and one of the largest observed HCN/CO radial declines.

quotes.quote: It also covers strongly barred galaxies (NGC 2903, NGC 3627), flocculent disks (NGC 5055, NGC 6946), strong nuclear bursts (NGC 2903, NGC 4321, NGC 6946), Virgo cluster members (NGC 4254, NGC 4321), and interacting galaxies (NGC 3627, NGC 5194).

The HCN intensity peaks at the center of each target and then appears prominent along the spiral arms (e.g., NGC 5055, NGC 5194 and NGC 6946) and central bars (e.g., NGC 2903 and NGC 3627). Globally, we find the brightest emission in NGC 6946 and the weakest in NGC 3184.

We observe the largest HCN/CO declines in NGC 3627 ( 0.60dex), NGC 4254 ( 0.60dex), NGC 5194 ( 0.60dex) and NGC 6946 ( 0.55dex). Again, we see no strong morphological divide, NGC 3627 and NGC 6946 are strongly barred galaxies with prominent dense gas emission in their centers and bars, while NGC 4254 and NGC 5194 are unbarred galaxies rich in molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 to connect H I disk thickness with holes breaking into the circumgalactic medium, while also identifying its low axial ratio as superthin-like.

quotes.quote: For example, boomsma08 detected a large number of holes and high-velocity gas in the typical spiral galaxy NGC 6946 (which is also present in our sample galaxy). They found the average diameter of the holes to be 1.2 kpc, whereas the diameter of the smallest hole is 766 pc. For NGC 6946, we find that the scale height reaches up to 300 pc at a radius of 12 kpc. This means that the thickness of the disc in NGC 6946 cannot contain the holes entirely, and most probably, they have broken out into the immediate environment of the circumgalactic medium (CGM). To further investigate the effectiveness of the holes in NGC 6946 in breaking out into the CGM, we calculate the fractional density at the edge of the holes in the vertical direction.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 6946’s magnetic arms and prior hot-gas findings with NGC 628, noting similar star-forming contrast and reconnection-heating diagnostics.

quotes.quote: To test this hypothesis, we analysed the radio and X-ray data for the grand-design spiral galaxy NGC 6946 wezgowiec16 , in which magnetic arms are present beck07 . The comparison of the properties of the hot gas and the magnetic fields in the spiral arms and the inter-arm regions showed a slight increase in the temperature of the hot gas (and the corresponding thermal energy) in the inter-arm regions that was accompanied by lower energy densities and by a higher order of the magnetic fields wezgowiec16 .

epord ), however, is higher in the magnetic arms than in the spiral arms, as observed before in NGC 6946 wezgowiec16 and M 83 wezgowiec20 . It resembles the former galaxy more because similarly to NGC 6946, the distribution of the star-forming regions in NGC 628 presents a high contrast between the spiral arms and the inter-arm regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as an SABc galaxy with specified inclination, position angle, optical radius, distance, and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 is SABc galaxy (morphological type code T = 5.9 0.3). The inclination angle of NGC 6946 is i = 33 , and the position angle of the major axis PA = 243 deBlok2008 . The optical radius is 5.74 arcmin RC3 . At the distance of d = 7.34 Mpc Anand2021 , the physical optical radius is R_ 25 = 12.26 kpc. The stellar mass (mean value) is M _ = 2.85 10 ^ 10 M _ Jarrett2019,Leroy2019 .
