sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as a mock PRIMA polarimetry target, noting its FIR size, radio/FIR inputs, and potential disk magnetic-field measurements.

quotes.quote: Simulated Science Target As an illustrative test case of our polarimetric mapmaking techniques, we consider a mock observation of the nearby face-on spiral galaxy NGC 6946. Its FIR size of 100 arcmin ^2 Aniano:2020 is well-matched to mapping with PPI while a wealth of ancillary data, including FIR and radio imaging and polarimetry, can inform our signal model.

For the Stokes Q and U maps, we start from 6 cm observations of NGC 6946 with the Very Large Array FIR Q and U maps of NGC 6946 were made with SOFIA Borlaff2023 , but these maps have lower signal-to-noise ratio over less area of the galaxy than the radio data and so are less suitable for simulation purposes.
For NGC 6946 specifically, the magnetic field could be mapped, and the dust polarization spectrum measured, throughout the 20 kpc disc for the first time.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as a standard spiral galaxy, estimating its inner-disk magnetic, gas, and cosmic-ray energy densities and emphasizing approximate equipartition in the neutral gas and magnetic field.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 (Fig. 2) is a useful standard spiral galaxy because it has no companion, no strong density waves, no active nucleus and has been observed in many spectral ranges. The average total equipartition field strength in the inner disk (within 4 kpc radius) is B_t 18 G (using the map of synchrotron emission by Walshetal2002 ) and the average density of the warm ionized gas ( T 10^4  K) is n_e 0.5  cm ^ 3 (using their map of thermal radio emission and a filling factor of 5 ). The map of the total neutral gas (molecular + atomic, where the molecular gas dominates until about 5 kpc radius) yields a mean number density (within a disk of 4 kpc radius and 100 pc height) of n 20  H atoms cm ^ 3 . The turbulent velocity of the neutral gas is assumed to be v_ turb = 7.5  km/s, as for the cold neutral gas in our Galaxy ( KalberlaandKerp1998 ), and the global rotation velocity v_ rot = 170  km/s.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 6946 as a face-on spiral modeled for radial dust temperature and dust heating, contrasting old-star and young-star contributions to its infrared emission.

quotes.quote: A related test utilising profiles rather than integrated quantities has been done for the radial temperature distribution, namely by comparing that predicted by the model of Bianchi et al. for NGC 6946 with that observed for the Milky Way (see Fig. 11, right panel).

In the modelling of the face-on spiral NGC 6946, Bianchi et al. found that it is the old stellar population that predominantly powers the dust emission, with only 40 of the emission powered by UV photons. However this result assumed that more than 50 of the absorbed UV radiation is channeled directly into the MIR, whereas self-consistent calculations of this percentage show that most of the UV luminosity is actually re-emitted in the FIR. For the same galaxy both Silva et al. and Sauty et al. found that most of the dust heating comes from the young stellar population.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6946 as a nearby spiral hosting a young super-star cluster and discusses its disk oxygen gradient, local abundance at the cluster radius, and possibly elevated current SFR.

quotes.quote: br92 measured O abundances for H ii regions distributed throughout the disk of NGC 6946 (using narrow-band imaging of O, N and H emission lines) and derived an oxygen abundance gradient of (O/H) / R = -0.089 0.003 dex kpc ^ 1 and a central value of 12 + (O/H) = 9.37. At the projected galactocentric distance of NGC6946-1447 (4.8 kpc), this corresponds to 12 + (O/H) = 8.94 0.01 . kob98 quote an O abundance of 12 + (O/H) = 9.13 at a radius of 3 kpc, or 12 + (O/H) = 8.97 at 4.8 kpc if we adopt the abundance gradient from br92 .
Alternatively, the current global SFR in NGC 6946 may be sufficiently elevated above the past average to cause a general enrichment of the ISM with elements.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as a grand-design spiral with strong total and regular magnetic fields, and magnetic energy exceeding ionized-gas energy density in its interstellar medium.

quotes.quote: Radio-faint galaxies like M 31 and M 33 have B_ t 6 G, while 15 G is typical for grand-design galaxies like M 51, M 83 and NGC 6946.
Exceptionally strong regular fields are detected in the interarm regions of NGC 6946 of up to 13 G (Beck Hoernes \ ) and 15 G at the inner edge of the inner spiral arms in M 51 (Fletcher et al. \ ).

In NGC 6946, the energy density of the magnetic field E_ magn (assuming energy density equipartition with cosmic rays) is one order of magnitude larger than that of the ionized gas E_ th (assuming a volume filling factor of 5 ). In the inner parts of NGC 6946, the energy densities of the total magnetic field and of turbulent gas motions are similar, but the field energy dominates in the outer parts due to the large radial scale length of the magnetic energy ( 8 kpc), compared to the scale length of about 3 kpc for the neutral gas density.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 6946 as a nearby spiral whose molecular gas morphology, kinematics, S-shaped inflow, and central gas concentration support repetitive nuclear-cluster growth.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:n6946 shows the molecular gas distribution in the nearby spiral NGC 6946. Both the morphology and the kinematics of the gas can be well explained by the effects of a small-scale stellar bar. The S-shaped flow pattern onto the nucleus and the large ( 1.6 10^7 ) gas concentration in the inner 10 lend credibility to the ``repetitive burst'' scenario for NC growth.

sources.summary: The paper surveys NGC 6946 for planetary nebulae, reports 71 candidates in the six-galaxy sequence, and derives a 6.1 Mpc PNLF distance consistent with previous work.

quotes.quote: As the first step to determine disk mass-to-light ratios for normal spiral galaxies, we present the results of an imaging survey for planetary nebulae (PNe) in six nearby, face-on systems: IC 342, M74 (NGC 628), M83 (NGC 5236), M94 (NGC 4736), NGC 5068, and NGC 6946. Using Blanco/Mosaic II and WIYN/OPTIC, we identify 165, 153, 241, 150, 19, and 71 PN candidates, respectively, and use the Planetary Nebula Luminosity Function (PNLF) to obtain distances. For M74 and NGC 5068, our distances of 8.6 0.3 Mpc and 5.4^ +0.2 _ 0.4 Mpc are the first reliable estimates to these objects; for IC 342 ( 3.5 0.3 Mpc), M83 ( 4.8 0.1 Mpc), M94 ( 4.4^ +0.1 _ 0.2 Mpc), and NGC 6946 ( 6.1 0.6 Mpc) our values agree well with those in the literature.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6946 as a grand-design barred spiral with a large oval, primary bar, nuclear bar, and resonance structure consistent with earlier analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 is a grand-design barred spiral galaxy with three main gravitational distortions: a large oval with radius R 4.5 , a R 1 \ primary stellar bar with ellipticity 0.15, and a R 8 \ nuclear bar with ellipticity 0.4, which is almost perpendicular to the main bar. We have presented a detailed analysis of the resonance structure in this galaxy in Fathietal2007TW , and confirm that the maps presented in this paper are fully consistent with our previous work.

In six galaxies (IC 342, NGC 2403, NGC 4294, NGC 4519, NGC 6946, NGC 7479, and NGC 7741), we have directly derived the \ of the main bar, and in four galaxies (NGC 4519, NGC 5371, NGC 5921, and NGC 5964) we have been able to derive the \ of the spiral arms.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6946 as a late-type spiral whose HI structures, low inclination, and uncertain velocity assumptions make its dynamics notable.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946. Although well known for its population of HI structures, the dynamics of NGC 6946, a M_B = -20.61 late type spiral at D = 5.9 \ Mpc , has been relatively little studied because of its low inclination ( i = 33^o , P.A. = 243^o ). The offset between the THINGS data we use here and previous determinations C90,Boo07 is likely due to different assumptions for i . Although quite limited in number, such a small sample is nevertheless ideal for our test of the HL potential.

sources.summary: The paper adds a 2_11-2_12 H2CO absorption detection to the previously reported 1_10-1_11 discovery in NGC 6946 and cites compact nuclear molecular structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946: In Mangum2008 we reported the discovery of the H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 transition in absorption. We add to this the H _2 CO 2_ 11 2_ 12 transition, also in absorption (Figure fig:Arp220NGC6946FormSpec ). Schinnerer2006 and Schinnerer2007 imaged the CO J= 1-0 , J= 2-1 , and HCN J= 1-0 emission from this galaxy, detecting a compact nuclear source size of 2^ and a ``nuclear spiral'' structure 5^ 10^ in size.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 6946 as a high-star-formation galaxy whose ISM pressure balance differs from the Milky Way, with magnetic pressure dominating ionized-gas pressure and a low-beta plasma state.

quotes.quote: discusses observations and analysis of the galaxy NGC 6946, characterized by a high star formation rate, and reports results on the variation of all pressures with galactocentric distance. finds that the magnetic pressure is considerably larger than the value quoted above for the Milky Way, and that the ionized gas pressure is approximately an order of magnitude less than magnetic pressure. The interstellar plasma of NGC 6946 appears to be a low- plasma, and the processes of energy equilibration have not progressed to completion.

sources.summary: The paper corrects NGC 6946 for strong foreground extinction, probes central and extranuclear regions, and finds a fairly uniform dust-attenuation relation with bright outer-disk H II regions.

quotes.quote: Only NGC 6946 falls in a region of the sky with large foreground extinction due to the Milky Way, which we correct for in our spectral data cube (see Section sec:linemaps ).

We also find these two measures to be complementary in the outer disk, for example with the extranuclear pointing in NGC 6946, where bright H ii regions allow measurement of the Balmer decrement although the stellar continuum light is negligible (filled black).
NGC 6946, for which we probe both the central and an extra nuclear region at 0.4 R _ 25 , also displays a fairly uniform relation throughout the galaxy.

sources.summary: The GBT observations reveal a low-column-density filament apparently connecting NGC 6946 to companions, with properties consistent with either a cold flow or tidal debris from satellite interaction.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, the \ observations of NGC 6946 reveal a filamentary feature apparently connecting NGC 6946 with its nearest companions. This \ filament has 5 10 ^ 18 \ and a FWHM of 55 5 and was invisible in past interferometer observations. The properties of this filament are broadly consistent with being a cold flow or debris from a past tidal interaction between NGC 6946 and its satellites.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 6946 as a nearby galaxy with strong magnetic energy density, locally high ordered magnetic fields, and magnetic spiral arms in its large-scale field.

quotes.quote: The magnetic field energy density is indeed comparable to that of the turbulent gas motion and much higher than that of the thermal gas as has been determined for the nearby galaxies NGC 6946 beck07 and M33 taba+08 .
The strength of the ordered magnetic fields in spiral galaxies are typically 1--5 G, and may reach locally values up to 10 - 15 G as e.g. in NGC 6946 beck07 and M51 fletcher+2011 .

Sometimes, the interarm region is filled smoothly with regular fields, in other cases the large-scale field form long filaments of polarized intensity like in IC342 (Figure ic342 ) krause93 or so-called magnetic spiral arms like in NGC 6946 beck+96 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 6946 has magnetic field strength correlated with surface star-formation rate and compares its magnetic, thermal, and turbulent gas energy densities across star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: We find that the magnetic field strength is strongly correlated with the surface star formation rate in the galaxies NGC 6946 and NGC 5236. Further, we compare the magnetic field energy density to the total (thermal + turbulent) energy densities of gas (neutral + ionized) to identify regions of efficient field amplification in the galaxy NGC 6946. We find that in regions of efficient star formation, the magnetic field energy density is comparable to that of the total energy density of various interstellar medium components and systematically dominates in regions of low star formation efficiency.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC6946 as the only external galaxy with AME detected, with significant emission in two of ten spiral-arm star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: Strangely, AME has only been detected in one external galaxy, NGC6946 Murphy2010,Scaife2010a (and possibly SMC/LMC). In fact, it was only significantly detected in two out of ten star forming regions within the spiral arms of NGC6946. It is possible that AME may only be strongly emitted in a small volume of an entire galaxy, and thus the integrated contribution of AME could be small in general.

sources.summary: The chapter uses NGC 6946 as an example of Faraday tomography, combining WSRT and shorter-wavelength data to model depolarization and estimate magnetic-field structure across its ISM.

quotes.quote: For example, a combined observation of NGC 6946 using the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) at =13 cm , 18 cm, and 22 cm, together with 3.5 cm and 6.2 cm data from beck_etal_2007 , is being used to model the depolarization see Figure figure:depol williams_inprep , probe new Faraday depths with RM Synthesis, and recover detailed estimates of the magnetic field structure across the entire galaxy. Assuming typical ISM properties and a simple configuration of a large-scale magnetic field along the line of sight and turbulent magnetic cells of equal size and uniform distribution, the Burn slab model burn_1966,sokoloff_etal_1998 can also be utilized to begin to tease out the coherence length of the large-scale line of sight magnetic field and correlation lengths of line of sight random magnetic fields. Applying this rather simple model to all of the various ISM environments in NGC 6946 is a stepping stone toward robustly estimating small scale magnetic field properties and relating those to other ISM tracers. Comparing these results to observations that trace other ISM properties, including star formation and \ line-width profiles, illuminates the roles played by both large- and small-scale magnetic fields in various dynamical processes within galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 6946 has a shallow kinetic-energy power spectrum, complicated nested-bar and nuclear-starburst structure, and large nearly uniform velocity-map regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 features an even shallower E(k) , with 0.8 . This galaxy also has a complicated dynamical structure, with three nested bars, a double molecular disc and a nuclear starburst region Romeo:2015aa . The velocity map of NGC 6946 see Fig. 65, bottom left panel, of THINGSpaper shows large structures, with sizes of 5 or greater, with little variation ( v 25 kms^ 1 ) in velocity. These relatively uniform regions could possibly account for the shallower shape of E_ KE,2D (k) .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6946 among high-SFR galaxies, contrasts earlier two-band photometry with a new five-band 16,000-source catalog, and plots it in the high-SFR CMD set.

quotes.quote: These catalogs include the highly star-forming galaxies M 83 , NGC 6946 , M 101 and M 51 (M 51 a and M 51 b) which enabled the first-ever identification of extragalactic candidate analogs of the Galactic stellar behemoth Carinae ref:Khan_2015c .

Likewise, ref:Khan_2010 reported two Spitzer band measurements of <6,000 objects in NGC 6946 whereas the catalog for this galaxy presented here contains five Spitzer band measurements for 16,000 sources.

Figure fig:cmd1 shows the m_ 4.5 vs. m_ 3.6 m_ 4.5 color magnitude diagram (CMD) for M 31 and Figure fig:cmd4 shows the same for the galaxies M 83 , NGC 6946 , M 101 and M 51 which have the highest SFR among all the galaxies surveyed.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 6946 as one of two GBT-observed disk galaxies, noting beam limitations and a newly detected filament linking it to companion galaxies.

quotes.quote: Of particular note, Pisano (2014) used the Green Bank Telescope (GBT) to reach down to \ 1.2 10^ 18 cm ^ 2 (5 ) in two disk galaxies, NGC 6946 and NGC 2997. Although the single-dish GBT has a large beam (a width of 16 and 32 kpc in NGC 6946 and NGC 2997, respectively), it does not suffer from the spatial filtering of interferometer observations that make them insensitive to emission on large angular scales. NGC 6946 was found to have a previously undetected filament connecting it to its companion galaxies, while NGC 2997 exhibited an extended \ disk with an outer radius R 53 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper says distance differences could affect NGC 6946 rotation-curve work, rejects its inner velocity-dispersion measurements, and notes its bulge reduces inner scale height.

quotes.quote: For some galaxies (DDO 154, IC 2574, NGC 5055, NGC 6946, and NGC 7793), the difference between the two distances is not negligible and could slightly influence the rotation curve.
For NGC 6946, we rejected the inner five velocity dispersion measurements after a comparison with the velocity dispersion profile of , who found that _ HI 12-15 for the central radii.
The presence of the bulge (NGC 2841, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, NGC 6946, and NGC 7331) reduces the scale height in the innermost regions.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 6946 has a north-south radio-continuum disk asymmetry; including losses most strongly reduces its model normalization, while diffusion dominates its cosmic-ray electron transport.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 has a north-south asymmetry with a radio-bright southern and a radio-dim northern half of the galactic disk.
The most prominent effect is observed in NGC 6946 where the losses lead to a two times smaller Q without changing the smoothing lengthscale.
Diffusion is the main transport mechanism in unperturbed galactic disks (NGC 6946, M 51, and the central region of NGC 4321).

sources.summary: NGC6946 is described as a large grand-design spiral with moderate star formation, compact outer-disk H II regions, non-declining outer H I velocity dispersion, and important extended-disk dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: The galaxies NGC628, NGC6946 and NGC3184 are grand design spirals with moderate star formation rates (SFRs) of values 1 to 5, but NGC4214 has a much lower SFR of 0.05 walter.etal.2008 .
Note that the large galaxies, and especially NGC6946, clearly show non-declining H i velocity dispersion in the outer parts of their disks.
For NGC6946, the difference between _ dyn and _ H i is not very significant but since the disk is very extended, the total dynamical mass beyond R_ 25 does become important.

sources.summary: The paper performs a new 21-cm kinematic analysis for NGC 6946 and says its observed energy may require warm neutral medium thermal energy.

quotes.quote: For NGC 6946, we performed a new kinematic analysis using the 21-cm data cube in with spatial resolution of 13 (i.e.

However, our model cannot fully reproduce the observed energy in the region 2 kpc R 5 kpc of DDO 154 and in the outskirts of IC 2574 and NGC 6946, indicating that some contribution from the thermal energy of the WNM may be required.
Let us take for example NGC 6946, which requires a significant contribution from the thermal energy of the warm atomic gas to explain the observed energy per unit area for R 10 kpc (see Fig.

sources.summary: NGC 6946 is one of the galaxies with the highest predicted chance of CTA detection, and its spectrum is shown among the paper's top-six candidate spectra.

quotes.quote: We identify galaxies of the highest chance of detection by CTA, including NGC 5236, M33, NGC 6946, and IC 342.
The spectra of the top six galaxies (NGC 5236, M33, NGC 6946, IC 342, NGC 2146, and NGC 7331) are shown in Figure fig:spectrum_examples .
There is a good chance of detection for NGC 5236, M33, NGC 6946, and IC 342.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as a contrasting spiral outside the Local Sheet, arguing its observers would less easily perceive the sheet-like galaxy distribution seen from the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: Challenges The spiral galaxy NGC 6946 looks much like the Milky Way, and it seems likely to be an equally good home for observers such as us. Since NGC 6946 is some 5 Mpc from the Local Sheet an observer in this galaxy would not so readily make out the Local Sheet of galaxies that we observe in the angular distribution of galaxies shown in Figure fig:LU . Are large spirals typically in sheet-like distributions, as we are, or might the sparser distribution of galaxies around NGC 6946 be more typical?

sources.summary: The study treats NGC 6946 as an isolated, nearly face-on spiral whose radio-bright interarm regions are best explained by cosmic-ray diffusion or streaming.

quotes.quote: We identified CR diffusion or streaming as the physical causes of radio-bright regions of unperturbed symmetric spiral galaxies as NGC 6946.
The radio-bright regions in NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:rcfir_spixx1c1_nice_5 ) correspond to the interarm regions and are thus most probably caused by CR diffusion or streaming.
The FP--radio/SFR correlation of NGC 6946 is very weak ( =0.3 ) but significantly higher than the PI-radio/SFR correlation.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6946 as an observational application, describing interlaced optical and magnetic spiral arms extending into its outer disc and comparing them to stationary MHD spiral patterns.

quotes.quote: In this final section we outline some potential applications of our analysis in the context of a nearby spiral galaxy NGC 6946, and then summarize our theoretical results and suggestions.
The interlacing of optical and magnetic spiral arms in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 6946 was first revealed by Beck Hoernes (1996) in the inner disc portion with an almost rigid rotation.

However, several wavelet analysis on multi-wavelength data of NGC 6946 (e.g. Frick et al. 2000, 2001) further revealed large-scale spiral arms extended well into the outer disc portion with a more or less flat rotation curve (e.g. Tacconi Young 1989; Sofue 1996; Ferguson et al. 1998), that seems to challenge earlier arguments that SMDWs are largely confined within the inner disc portion of almost rigid rotation.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 6946 has faint ancient cluster candidates whose luminosity distribution may rise beyond the normal turnover, resembling M101 more than M81.

quotes.quote: Ancient clusters in M101 and possibly in NGC 6946, two of the three later-type spirals in our sample, appear to have luminosity distributions which continue to rise to our detection limit ( M_V 6.0 ), well beyond the expected turnover ( M_V 7.4 ) in the luminosity function.

The faint ancient cluster candidates in M101 and NGC 6946 have properties (colors and r_ eff ) similar to their more luminous counterparts, and we suggest that these are either intermediate age ( 3-9 Gyr) disk clusters or the low mass end of the original GC population.

Although the GC catalog for NGC 6946 contains relatively few objects, we note that the luminosity distribution appears more similar to that for M101 GCs rather than M81 GCs, due to the apparent ``excess'' of clusters beyond the expected turnover of M_V 7.4 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as having high-velocity gas clouds tied to H I disk holes, with possible galactic-fountain or infalling-primordial-gas explanations.

quotes.quote: In NGC 6946, many clouds of \ are seen moving at velocities inconsistent with the rotation of the galaxy (Kamphuis Sancisi 1993). This high-velocity gas is widely associated with holes in the \ disk, and may be explained as gas that was ejected via a galactic fountain powered by supernovae in the galaxy. An alternative explanation is that these clouds are infalling primordial material which has punched holes in the galaxy (Kamphuis Sancisi 1993).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 6946 as a clear nuclear gas-fueling case, describing a CO molecular-gas map, a spiral-like gas distribution, an inner bar, and ongoing central star formation.

quotes.quote: Another clear case of gas fueling of the nuclear regions is provided by the high resolution ^ 12 CO(2-1) map of NGC 6946 (Schinnerer et al., in preparation; Fig. fig:n6946 ). The spiral-like distribution of the molecular gas is reminiscent of the dust lanes observed in barred galaxies, and indeed a small inner bar has been detected via K band imaging in this galaxy (Elmegreen et al. 1998). The amount of gas which is fueled within the central 20 pc is uncertain, but star formation is already ongoing there, which may thus lead to the formation of a flattened nuclear disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6946 as a nearby starburst galaxy with a bright elongated central molecular feature, outer wispy arms, enhanced central ratios, and compact nuclear radio excess.

quotes.quote: NGC 6946 . This nearby starburst galaxy has an elongated, bright north-south molecular feature in the inner 50 \ and wispy arms at larger radii. The \ image (Figure figuraq ) and profile (Figure 1 i ) show an enhanced ratio within the central region, apart from the compact HII region nucleus, which shows relatively enhanced RC emission. Like IC 342, it may be that this region is a large-scale analog to active starforming regions in the Milky Way.

gmw_00000291.name: NGC 128
gmw_00000291.overview: NGC 128 is an interacting lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1603.00019, arXiv:2003.00015] featuring a pronounced boxy, peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:1603.00019, arXiv:1505.00590] with nested peanut structures [arXiv:2003.00015]. It hosts a warped stellar disk [arXiv:2003.00015] and a counter-rotating, tilted ionized gas disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0512143, arXiv:2003.00015] that is tidally interacting with its companion galaxy NGC 127 [arXiv:2003.00015, arXiv:1202.4790].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 128 as its strong-peanut demonstration case, finding a pronounced X/P structure, two bar components, and two co-existing associated peanuts.

quotes.quote: Here we demonstrate our analysis of X/P bulges on the interesting, well known peanut galaxy NGC 128. This peculiar lenticular galaxy displays a very pronounced X/P structure (Figure fig:n128_SDSS ) and is in fact the first galaxy where such a feature was noted ( BurbidgeBurbidge1959 ). The first image that we modelled was the SDSS band image.

The decomposition of NGC 128's surface brightness profile is shown in the top panel of Figure fig:n128_decomp . In addition to the spheroid component (red curve) and inclined disc component (blue curve), the profile also displays the signatures of two bar components, plotted in cyan. For NGC 128 though, as we will show below, each of the two co-existing bars has its own corresponding peanut.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 128 as a well-studied S0 with a boxy/peanut bulge, counter-rotating tilted ionized gas, and interactions with nearby group companions.

quotes.quote: We have identified two new galaxies with gas counter-rotation (NGC 1596 and NGC 3203) and have confirmed similar behaviour in another one (NGC 128), this using results from separate studies of the ionized-gas and stellar kinematics of a well-defined sample of 30 edge-on disc galaxies.

first discussed the stellar rotation curve of NGC 128 and detected its ``double-hump'' structure, nicely confirmed by . Although ionized-gas emission had already been detected, first pointed out that it is counter-rotating. and published comprehensive studies of the structure and stellar/ionized-gas kinematics of NGC 128, confirming the counter-rotation of the ionized gas and showing that it is tilted by 25 - 40 with respect to the main (equatorial) plane of the galaxy see also mefpt95,opcm97 .
NGC 128 is in a compact group and is clearly interacting with its neighbors NGC 127 (SA0 ^0 ; V 190 km s ^ 1 ) and NGC 130 (SA0 ^- ; V 190 km s ^ 1 ). briefly explored the dynamics of the triplet.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 128 has nested peanut features with a low scale ratio but avoids using it as the analogue because it is a perturbed interacting S0 with outlier peanut strength.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, two galaxies in this sample, NGC 128 and NGC 2549, were found to host a double, nested peanut configuration, i.e., a small scale X/P bulge embedded within a larger one. While only recently simulations of barred galaxies are seen to develop double X--shaped features under certain circumstances (e.g., Debattista+2017 , Smirnov&Sotnikova2018 , Parul+2020 ), the face-value interpretation of CG16 at the time was that each of the two co-existing X/P structures is associated with a distinct bar component, as double bar galaxies had been known for long to occur ( Friedli+1996 , Erwin+2001 , Erwin2011 ). Indeed, in a recent paper have detected a double peanut configuration in the face--on galaxy NGC 1291 (using the face - on signature of peanut galaxies, as introduced in Debattista+2005 ), which indeed hosts a large--scale, and a second embedded nuclear, bar. Nevertheless, the scale ratio of the two peanuts in NGC 128 and especially in NGC 2549 ( 3:1 ) is rather low for a (large scale+nuclear) bar configuration, and thus warrants further investigation.

Although NGC 128 was also found to host nested peanut features in the aforementioned paper, we refrain to compare our simulation with this object, as this is a strongly perturbed, peculiar lenticular galaxy (S0 pec) with an offset, counter-rotating gas disc and a visibly warped stellar disc, discernibly interacting -- exchanging mass -- with its companion NGC 127 ( Emsellem&Arsenault1997 and references therein). The latter process very likely contributes to the peanut instability, and indeed leads to outlier peanut parameters, particularly strength.
The grey symbols correspond to NGC 128 (downward triangles) and NGC 2549 (upward triangles), filled for the outer and empty for the inner peanut, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 128 as the gas-rich central S0 of the group, with a peanut-shaped bulge, counter-rotating gas, and apparent gas transfer toward NGC 127.

quotes.quote: The NGC 128 group (d=57 Mpc) includes (spectroscopically confirmed): giant gas-rich S0 NGC 128 with a peanut-shaped bulge, giant Sa NGC 125, 3 low-luminosity S0 closer than 100 kpc in projection to NGC 128: NGC 126 (M _B =-18.5, 61 kpc), NGC 127 (M _B =-18.6, 14 kpc), and NGC 130 (M _B =-19.0, 16 kpc), and several late-type spirals beyond 300 kpc. NGC 128 is known to have a gaseous disc counter-rotating to the stars (Emsellem Arsenault, 1997).

While NGC 126 and 130 look similar to cluster dS0/S0's, NGC 127 is a star-forming galaxy with unusual kinematics of gas and a bridge connecting it to NGC 128. We propose the following scenario: NGC 127 has recently passed its pericentre, and now we observe an infall of gas from NGC 128 onto NGC 127.

sources.summary: UGCA 292 has triple-peaked, boomerang-shaped H I mimicking the optical structure, H I peaks at disk/star-forming regions, and a velocity gradient offset from the gas and optical axes.

quotes.quote: UGCA 292 (also known as NGC 128) From Young et al. (2003; their Fig. 2), with the VLA, and Ott et al. (2012; their Fig. 22), from the VLA ANGST. Intermediate resolution (17.7 x 17.4 and 14.2 x 13.9 ) H 1 maps show triple-peaked, "boomerang-shaped" H 1 emission along an almost horizontal axis (PA _ HI 175 ^ ). The internal H 1 inverted "V-shape" structure mimics what is observed in the optical (PA _ opt 165 ^ ). Indeed, the H 1 peaks are positionally coincident with the ends and center of the optical disk, where bright star formation knots can be found. The vertical gradient direction (PA _ VG 145 ^ ) is misaligned by about 20 ^ from the overall H 1 and optical emission, but is roughly aligned with the Southwest "boomerang" optical/ H 1 arm.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 128 as an early-type barred counter-rotator with H I evidence and an optical stellar tail indicating tidal gas accretion from NGC 127.

quotes.quote: Draft Version firstpage - lastpage 2002 We probe the H i properties and the gas environments of three early-type barred galaxies harbouring counter-rotating ionized gas, NGC 128, NGC 3203 and NGC 7332.
In NGC 128 and NGC 3203, we find 9.6 10^7 and 2.3 10^8 M_ of H i , respectively, covering almost the entire stellar bodies of dwarf companions that appear physically connected.
At optical wavelengths, only one galaxy among the sample, NGC 128, shows direct evidence for tidal interaction with its neighbour NGC 127, as clearly indicated by the stellar tail between them.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 128 as an early recognized boxy-bulge galaxy whose cylindrical rotation and fast-rotating bulge argue against a dynamically hot bulge.

quotes.quote: The earliest notion of boxy bulges goes as far as to 1959, when the Burbidges recognized such a structure in NGC 128, which galaxy was later shown in the Hubble Atlas of Galaxies by sandage1961 .

Support for the scenario by Combes Sanders came from the observation that NGC 4565 and NGC 128, with obvious boxy bulges in morphology, appeared to have cylindrical rotation kormendyilling1982,kormendyilling1983 , which means that the rotational velocity depends only little on the vertical height from the equatorial plane.
It is also worth mentioning that bertola1977 had already shown that the bulge in NGC 128 is fast rotating and therefore cannot be dynamically hot.

gmw_00000292.name: NGC 126
gmw_00000292.overview: NGC 126 is a low-luminosity S0 galaxy located near NGC 128 [arXiv:0711.2100]. The galaxy contains a bar that displays an S-shaped stellar velocity field, and it shows no emission lines [arXiv:0711.2100].

sources.summary: The paper reports 3D spectroscopy of NGC 126, finding it is a low-luminosity S0 near NGC 128 with a prominent bar, S-shaped velocity field, and no emission lines.

quotes.quote: We have obtained observations for NGC 126, 127, and 130 in 2005 and 2006 with the MPFS IFU spectrograph at the 6-m telescope of SAO RAS. Using a novel stellar population fitting technique (Chilingarian et al. 2005, 2006, 2007a, Prugniel et al. 2005) we have derived maps of the stellar population parameters (age and metallicity) and internal kinematics of stars and ionised gas. NGC 126 contains a prominent bar seen on direct images. It appears as a S-shaped structure on the velocity field. No emission lines are seen in the fitting residuals.

gmw_00000293.name: NGC 127
gmw_00000293.overview: NGC 127 is a gas-rich, early-type galaxy with ongoing star formation [arXiv:0711.2100]. It is interacting with its neighbor NGC 128, as evidenced by a connecting stellar bridge [arXiv:0711.2100, arXiv:1202.4790] and gas accretion between the two systems [arXiv:0711.2100, arXiv:1202.4790].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 127 as a gas-rich, star-forming early-type galaxy with asymmetric ionized-gas kinematics, a bridge to NGC 128, and a likely recent pericentre passage.

quotes.quote: NGC 127 is a gas-rich object with early-type morphology and ongoing star formation: strong emission lines are observed. H has been used to recover velocity field of the ionised gas showing faster rotation ( 100 km/s) than stars ( 50 km/s), and asymmetry on the SE part of NGC 127 (direction of NGC 128) that may be caused by perturbed motions of the gas near the region, where the accreting flow reaches NGC 127.

While NGC 126 and 130 look similar to cluster dS0/S0's, NGC 127 is a star-forming galaxy with unusual kinematics of gas and a bridge connecting it to NGC 128. We propose the following scenario: NGC 127 has recently passed its pericentre, and now we observe an infall of gas from NGC 128 onto NGC 127. After gas removal and several Gyr of passive evolution it will fade down and would be indistinctive from ''normal'' dE galaxies by morphology and luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 127 as NGC 128's interacting companion, with H I covering most of its stellar body and likely being accreted onto NGC 128.

quotes.quote: At optical wavelengths, only one galaxy among the sample, NGC 128, shows direct evidence for tidal interaction with its neighbour NGC 127, as clearly indicated by the stellar tail between them.
As shown on the right panel of Figure fig-n0128hi , the H i is found only on one half of the disc, covering almost the entire stellar disc of its neighbour, NGC 127.
The H i morphology and the velocity profile strongly suggest that the H i was originally bound to NGC 127 rather than NGC 128.

gmw_00000294.name: NGC 130
gmw_00000294.overview: NGC 130 is a low-luminosity S0 galaxy characterized by a relatively old stellar population and regular kinematics [arXiv:0711.2100]. It lacks ionized gas but hosts a spatially unresolved young nucleus [arXiv:0711.2100].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 130 as a low-luminosity S0 with old stellar populations, regular kinematics, no ionized gas, and an unresolved young nucleus reminiscent of Virgo dE galaxies.

quotes.quote: NGC 130 located at nearly the same projected distance is very different. Relatively old stellar population, regular kinematics and absence of ionised gas suggest it is located much further from NGC 128. NGC 130 exhibits a spatially unresolved young nucleus ( t=4 Gyr) reminiscent of young central structures observed in bright Virgo cluster dE galaxies (Chilingarian et al. 2007b).

gmw_00000295.name: Scl-MM-Dw2
gmw_00000295.overview: Scl-MM-Dw2 is a diffuse dwarf galaxy located approximately 3.12 Mpc away, likely as a satellite of NGC 253 [arXiv:1512.03816]. It hosts an old, metal-poor stellar population and is highly elongated, suggesting it is currently undergoing tidal disruption [arXiv:1512.03816].

sources.summary: The paper discovers Scl-MM-Dw2 near NGC 253, measuring its distance, old metal-poor population, gas poverty, large diffuse shape, and evidence that it is tidally disrupting.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of Scl-MM-Dw2, a new dwarf galaxy at a projected separation of 50 kpc from NGC 253, as part of the PISCeS (Panoramic Imaging Survey of Centaurus and Sculptor) project. We measure a tip of the red giant branch distance of 3.12 0.30  Mpc, suggesting that Scl-MM-Dw2 is likely a satellite of NGC 253. We qualitatively compare the distribution of red giant branch (RGB) stars in the color-magnitude diagram with theoretical isochrones and find that it is consistent with an old, 12 Gyr, and metal poor, 2.3<   [ Fe/H ]   <-1.1 , stellar population. We also detect a small number of asymptotic giant branch stars consistent with a metal poor 2-3  Gyr population in the center of the dwarf.

Scl-MM-Dw2 has a luminosity of M_V=-12.1 0.5  mag and a half-light radius of   =2.94 0.46  kpc which makes it moderately larger than dwarf galaxies in the Local Group of the same luminosity. However, Scl-MM-Dw2 is very elongated ( =0.66 0.06 ) and it has an extremely low surface brightness ( _ 0,V =26.5 0.7  mag arcsec ^ 2 ). Its elongation and diffuseness make it an outlier in the ellipticity-luminosity and surface brightness-luminosity scaling relations. These properties suggest that this dwarf is being tidally disrupted by NGC 253.
